data.title: Hut 8 Mining GAAP EPS of -$1.30, revenue of $21.8M
data.description:
• Hut 8 Mining press release (NASDAQ:HUT): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$1.30.
  • Revenue of $21.8M (-57.8% Y/Y).
  • Revenue of $21.8 million, net loss of $134.3 million, and Adjusted EBITDA of ($117.7) million.
  • Total energy capacity under management of 1,020 megawatts (“MW”) as of March 31, 2025.
  • ~10,800 MW development pipeline with ~2,600 MW of capacity under exclusivity as of March 31, 2025.
  • Strategic Bitcoin reserve of 10,264 Bitcoin with a market value of $847.2 million as of March 31, 2025.

data.title: BCE Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69, revenue of $5.93B
data.description:
• BCE press release (NYSE:BCE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69.
  • Revenue of $5.93B (-1.3% Y/Y).
  • Net earnings of $683 million, up 49.5% with net earnings attributable to common shareholders of $630 million, up 56.7% or $0.68 per common share
  • Adjusted net earnings1 of $633 million yielded adjusted EPS1 of $0.69, down 4.2%
  • Consolidated adjusted EBITDA1 essentially stable in Q1 2025 compared with Q1 2024 with 0.4 percentage-point increase in margin2 to 43.1% on 2.1% lower operating costs
  • Free cash flow1 increased $713 million to $798 million; cash flows from operating activities up 38.8% to $1,571 million
  • Bell Media revenue up 6.9% with 35.9% adjusted EBITDA growth; digital revenue3 up 12% as digital platforms and advertising technology continue to drive growth

data.title: Liquidia GAAP EPS of -$0.45 misses by $0.05, revenue of $3.12M misses by $0.11M
data.description:
• Liquidia press release (NASDAQ:LQDA): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.45 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $3.12M (+5.1% Y/Y) misses by $0.11M.
  • Revenue related primarily to the promotion agreement with Sandoz, Inc. pursuant to which we share profits from the sale of Treprostinil Injection in the United States (Promotion Agreement). The increase of $0.1 million was primarily due to the impact of unfavorable gross-to-net returns adjustments recorded in the prior year offset by lower sales volumes in the current year.

data.title: Playtika GAAP EPS of $0.08 misses by $0.03, revenue of $706M beats by $6.33M
data.description:
• Playtika press release (NASDAQ:PLTK): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.08 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $706M (+8.4% Y/Y) beats by $6.33M.
  • We reaffirm our guidance of revenue between $2.80 and $2.85 billion and Adjusted EBITDA between $715 and $740 million.

data.title: Universal is set to open the largest theme park of the last 20 years
data.description:
The largest theme park opening in the U.S. in over 20 years is scheduled for May 22, when the Universal Epic Universe opens at Universal Orlando Resort. Epic Universe features immersive worlds Super Nintendo World, the Wizarding World of Harry Potter: Ministry of Magic, How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk, Dark Universe, and Celestial Park, as well as three new hotels and over 50 attractions, restaurants, and shops.

The Epic Universe park itself covers about 110 acres, but the overall development spans a massive 750 acres, making it the largest Universal property and one of the biggest theme park projects globally.

Analysts have pointed to the park's opening as a potentially transformative moment for Universal and parent company Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA), with the Universal Orlando footprint set to double to raise the stakes in Central Florida's theme park wars. Epic Universe is expected to draw millions of new visitors, potentially shifting market share from Disney (DIS) as guests extend their stays or prioritize Universal’s innovative offerings. While Disney is responding with long-term investments in its parks portfolio, it lacks a major new opening this year and has faced some consumer backlash on pricing.

Early ticket sales for Epic Universe and preview reactions have exceeded expectations, signaling strong demand and a bright outlook for Universal's new theme park era.

Epic Universe will be the fourth theme park at Universal Orlando after Universal Studios Florida, Islands of Adventure, and water park Volcano Bay. Meanwhile, Disney (DIS) has four theme parks near Orlando, all part of Walt Disney World Resort (Magic Kingdom Park, EPCOT, Disney's Hollywood Studios, and Disney's Animal Kingdom Theme Park), while SeaWorld Orlando is operated by United Parks & Resorts (PRKS) and Merlin Entertainments operates LEGOLAND Florida Resort, Peppa Pig Theme Park in Winter Haven, and ICON Park.

data.title: Henkel AG reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Henkel AG press release (OTCPK:HENKY): Q1 Revenue of Є5.24B (-1.5% Y/Y).
  • Adhesive Technologies: positive organic sales growth of 1.1 percent with positive vol-ume development despite challenging environment
  • Consumer Brands: organic sales development of -3.5 percent; volumes impacted by subdued consumer demand and supply chain challenges
  • Sale of Retailer Brands business in North America closed – strategic portfolio optimization program in Consumer Brands concluded 
  • Outlook for fiscal 2025 unchanged despite increased volatility in marketenvironment:
    • Organic sales growth: 1.5 to 3.5 percent.
    • Adjusted return on sales: 14.0 to 15.5 percent.
    • Adjusted earnings per preferred share (EPS): increase in the low to high single-digit per-centage range (at constant exchange rates).
    • Restructuring expenses of 200 to 250 million euros.
    • Cash outflows from investments in property, plant and equipment and intangible assets of between 650 and 750 million euros.

data.title: Canadian Tire and WestJet launch strategic partnership
data.description:
Canadian Tire (CTC:CA) and WestJet Thursday announced a long-term strategic partnership that brings together Triangle Rewards and WestJet Rewards.

Launching in early 2026, this partnership will give Triangle Rewards' and WestJet Rewards' members the ability to link their loyalty accounts.

data.title: Allied Gold reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Allied Gold press release (OTCPK:AAUCF): non-GAAP EPS of $0.14.
  • EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2025, were $103.2M and $133.8M, respectively.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $232.3M.
